title: Biz Group Urges OECD To Clarify Transfer Pricing Updates - Law360
summary: Biz Group Urges OECD To Clarify Transfer Pricing Updates Law360
ai_summary: An unnamed 'Biz Group' has urged the OECD to provide further clarification on recent transfer pricing updates. This indicates ongoing engagement from stakeholders regarding the practical application and interpretation of OECD's latest guidance on transfer pricing matters.

title: A Competitive Corporate Tax Code is Key to Sustaining Strong Economic Growth
summary: The 12-year history of Tax Foundation’s ITCI shows that tax policy is constantly in flux around the world and that tax policy design choices matter for economic growth.
ai_summary: This article discusses how a competitive corporate tax code is essential for sustained economic growth, referencing the Tax Foundation's International Tax Competitiveness Index. It highlights the dynamic nature of global tax policy and its impact on economic outcomes.

title: ITAT Delhi Allows Sections 54/54F Exemption, Rejects Arbitrary FMV Determination as on 01.04.2001
summary: ITAT Delhi deleted the Section 54/54F disallowance, accepted the registered valuer’s FMV as on 01.04.2001, and rejected the AO’s valuation method. The post ITAT Delhi Allows Sections 54/54F Exemption, Rejects Arbitrary FMV Determination as on 01.04.2001 appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT Delhi allowed Section 54/54F exemption, accepting the valuation determined by a registered valuer as of April 1, 2001. The tribunal rejected the arbitrary valuation methodology employed by the Assessing Officer.

title: Registered Valuer’s FMV & Indexed Cost Accepted After AO Omitted DVO Reference: ITAT Delhi
summary: ITAT Delhi accepted the registered valuer's FMV and indexed cost after holding the AO could not reject the valuation without referring it to the DVO. The post Registered Valuer’s FMV & Indexed Cost Accepted After AO Omitted DVO Reference: ITAT Delhi appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT Delhi accepted the registered valuer's Fair Market Value (FMV) and indexed cost, ruling that the Assessing Officer could not reject the valuation without referring it to the Departmental Valuation Officer. This decision reinforces the procedural requirements for challenging valuations in India.

title: Ad Hoc Commission Cap Without Comparables Invalid; Section 40A(2)(b) Inapplicable: Bengaluru ITAT
summary: ITAT Bengaluru deleted ad hoc commission and interest disallowances, holding Section 40A(2)(b) inapplicable to interest paid to a non-related party. The post Ad Hoc Commission Cap Without Comparables Invalid; Section 40A(2)(b) Inapplicable: Bengaluru ITAT appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The Bengaluru ITAT ruled that an ad hoc commission cap imposed without proper comparables is invalid and held Section 40A(2)(b) inapplicable to interest paid to a non-related party. This decision underscores the importance of a 'comparables' based approach for assessing reasonableness in related-party transactions, aligning with transfer pricing principles even in a domestic context.

title: Section 69B Addition on Gold Stock Supported by Manufacturer’s Bill deleted: ITAT Visakhapatnam
summary: ITAT Visakhapatnam upheld deletion of the Section 69B addition for excess gold stock and sustained the ₹3.50 lakh addition relating to deficit cash. The post Section 69B Addition on Gold Stock Supported by Manufacturer’s Bill deleted: ITAT Visakhapatnam appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT Visakhapatnam upheld the deletion of an addition made under Section 69B for excess gold stock, confirming the validity of the manufacturer's bill. However, the tribunal sustained a ₹3.50 lakh addition related to deficit cash, highlighting specific issues of unexplained investments.

title: ITAT Pune Deletes Section 270A Penalty on Estimated Section 43CA Addition
summary: ITAT Pune deleted Section 270A penalty after holding the addition under Section 43CA was based on the DVOs estimated valuation. The post ITAT Pune Deletes Section 270A Penalty on Estimated Section 43CA Addition appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT Pune deleted a Section 270A penalty, ruling that the addition under Section 43CA was based on an estimated valuation from the Departmental Valuation Officer. This decision clarifies penalty implications where tax additions are based on estimated property valuations.

title: Crypto Futures Tax in India: How Speculative Treatment Can Reduce Tax
summary: Article explains possible tax treatment of crypto perpetual futures, comparing Sections 43(5), 73 and 115BBH, turnover, valuation and INR conversion. The post Crypto Futures Tax in India: How Speculative Treatment Can Reduce Tax appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: This article explores the possible tax treatment of crypto perpetual futures in India, comparing various sections of the Income Tax Act, including Sections 43(5), 73, and 115BBH. It discusses how speculative treatment could potentially reduce tax liabilities for crypto investors.

title: Webinar – Automating operational transfer pricing with EXA OTP: leveraging SAP Joule and AI agents - International Tax Review
summary: Webinar – Automating operational transfer pricing with EXA OTP: leveraging SAP Joule and AI agents International Tax Review
ai_summary: This webinar explores the automation of operational transfer pricing through EXA OTP, leveraging SAP Joule and AI agents. It highlights how artificial intelligence can streamline the implementation and management of transfer pricing policies within multinational enterprises.

title: Single-Gate Natural Resource Exports and Their Impact on Transfer Pricing - MUC Consulting
summary: Single-Gate Natural Resource Exports and Their Impact on Transfer Pricing MUC Consulting
ai_summary: This article from MUC Consulting discusses the complexities of transfer pricing for single-gate natural resource exports. It explores how specific industry dynamics, such as valuation and export mechanisms, impact the arm's length determination for such transactions.

title: Transfer pricing documentation and tax and customs-tax audits - RSM Global
summary: Transfer pricing documentation and tax and customs-tax audits RSM Global
ai_summary: RSM Global provides insights into the critical role of robust transfer pricing documentation in mitigating risks during tax and customs-tax audits. The analysis likely covers best practices for preparing and maintaining documentation to withstand scrutiny from tax authorities globally.

title: CIT cannot Cancel Section 12A Registration Before Section 12AA(3) Insertion: SC
summary: Supreme Court held the CIT had no power to cancel Section 12A registration before 01.10.2004, restored the ITAT order and set aside the High Court judgment. The post CIT cannot Cancel Section 12A Registration Before Section 12AA(3) Insertion: SC appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The Indian Supreme Court ruled that the Commissioner of Income Tax lacked the power to cancel Section 12A registration before October 1, 2004, when Section 12AA(3) was inserted. This decision restored an ITAT order and set aside a High Court judgment concerning charitable trusts.

title: Double Taxation Relief on Bonus Provision Reversal; Brokerage Provision Allowed; Section 80G Deduction Upheld for CSR Donations: ITAT Mumbai
summary: ITAT Mumbai deleted disallowances on bonus provision reversal and brokerage provision, allowed Section 80G deduction, and allowed the assessee's appeal. The post Double Taxation Relief on Bonus Provision Reversal; Brokerage Provision Allowed; Section 80G Deduction Upheld for CSR Donations: ITAT Mumbai appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT Mumbai allowed an assessee's appeal, deleting disallowances related to bonus provision reversal and brokerage provision. The tribunal also upheld the Section 80G deduction for Corporate Social Responsibility donations in this general income tax case.

title: Section 271AAB Penalty Deleted on Search Disclosure Additions: ITAT Visakhapatnam
summary: ITAT Visakhapatnam deleted Section 271AAB penalty, holding the Revenue failed to establish undisclosed income despite search disclosures. The post Section 271AAB Penalty Deleted on Search Disclosure Additions: ITAT Visakhapatnam appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT Visakhapatnam deleted a Section 271AAB penalty imposed on an assessee, ruling that the Revenue failed to establish undisclosed income despite search disclosures. This decision pertains to general income tax penalties in India.

title: ITAT Applies ₹200 Crore Turnover Filter, Revises TP Comparables: ITAT Chennai
summary: ITAT Chennai directed exclusion of high-turnover and functionally different comparables and inclusion of Rheal Software in TP benchmarking. The post ITAT Applies ₹200 Crore Turnover Filter, Revises TP Comparables: ITAT Chennai appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT Chennai issued a significant ruling on a transfer pricing case, directing the application of a ₹200 Crore turnover filter for comparable selection. The tribunal mandated the exclusion of high-turnover and functionally different comparables, while including Rheal Software for benchmarking analysis.

title: ITAT Excludes TP Comparables, Remands Overdue Receivables Adjustment: ITAT Delhi
summary: ITAT Delhi modified the TP comparable set, remanded overdue receivables and Form 26AS additions, and directed recomputation of Section 234C interest. The post ITAT Excludes TP Comparables, Remands Overdue Receivables Adjustment: ITAT Delhi appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT Delhi issued a ruling that modified the set of transfer pricing comparables for an assessee. The tribunal also remanded the issues of overdue receivables adjustment and Form 26AS additions, directing a recomputation of Section 234C interest.

title: Intra-group services costs and the benefit test – key changes in the proposed OECD guidelines - Deloitte
summary: Intra-group services costs and the benefit test – key changes in the proposed OECD guidelines Deloitte
ai_summary: This Deloitte article highlights key changes in the proposed OECD guidelines concerning intra-group services costs and the application of the benefit test. title: When an Arbitral Award Becomes State Aid - Aceris Law LLC
summary: When an Arbitral Award Becomes State Aid Aceris Law LLC
ai_summary: This article from Aceris Law LLC explores the circumstances under which an arbitral award might be classified as state aid. It delves into the intersection of arbitration law and state aid regulations, primarily relevant in the EU context.

title: Commission approves amendment to Italian State aid scheme to compensate energy-intensive companies for indirect emission costs - PubAffairs Bruxelles
summary: Commission approves amendment to Italian State aid scheme to compensate energy-intensive companies for indirect emission costs PubAffairs Bruxelles
ai_summary: The European Commission has approved an amendment to an Italian State aid scheme designed to compensate energy-intensive companies for indirect emission costs. This decision relates to EU competition law and national subsidy programs, without direct implications for transfer pricing.

title: Cash Deposits Already Offered Under Section 44AD Can’t Be Taxed Again Under Section 69A: ITAT Hyderabad
summary: ITAT Hyderabad deleted Section 69A addition as cash deposits were already accepted as business turnover under Section 44AD, avoiding double taxation. The post Cash Deposits Already Offered Under Section 44AD Can’t Be Taxed Again Under Section 69A: ITAT Hyderabad appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT Hyderabad ruled that cash deposits previously accepted as business turnover under Section 44AD cannot be taxed again under Section 69A, preventing double taxation. This domestic Indian tax ruling clarifies the application of presumptive taxation and unexplained cash credit provisions.

title: Romania resets its transfer pricing rulebook: why the new framework matters - International Tax Review
summary: Romania resets its transfer pricing rulebook: why the new framework matters International Tax Review
ai_summary: Romania has significantly updated its transfer pricing regulations, introducing a new framework that will impact multinational enterprises operating within the country. This new rulebook aims to align local practices with international standards, necessitating a review of current TP policies for compliance.

title: Transfer pricing of treasury transactions
summary: Transfer pricing of treasury transactions kpmg.com
ai_summary: This article discusses the transfer pricing considerations for various treasury transactions, such as intra-group loans, cash pooling, and financial guarantees. It highlights the importance of applying the arm's length principle to ensure compliance and manage financial risks for multinational corporations.

title: How global mobility is reshaping transfer pricing and PE risks - International Tax Review
summary: How global mobility is reshaping transfer pricing and PE risks International Tax Review
ai_summary: Global mobility trends are increasingly impacting both transfer pricing methodologies and the risk of creating permanent establishments (PEs) for multinational companies. The article examines how mobile workforces can trigger PE risks and complicate profit attribution, requiring companies to reassess their tax strategies.

title: Flawed EU Tax Disclosure Standards Will Cause Confusion
summary: The EU public country-by-country data provides information on taxes and profits that do not align with standard financial disclosure requirements.
ai_summary: The EU's public country-by-country reporting (CbCR) standards are criticized for their flawed design, leading to potential confusion for taxpayers and the public. The misalignment between CbCR data and standard financial disclosure requirements poses challenges for accurate interpretation of corporate tax contributions.

title: How Withholding Taxes Affect Cross-Border Investment in Europe
summary: Because withholding taxes can create double taxation and administrative friction even where foreign tax credits are available, the European Commission’s 2026 Tax Omnibus proposal to eliminate them on dividend, interest, and royalty payments between EU companies regardless of holding percentage would foster stronger cross-border savings and investment.
ai_summary: The European Commission's 2026 Tax Omnibus proposal aims to eliminate withholding taxes on dividend, interest, and royalty payments between EU companies. This initiative seeks to reduce double taxation and administrative burdens, thereby fostering greater cross-border investment and savings within the European Union.

title: AO Cannot Reject Assessee’s Chosen Rule 11UA Valuation Without Defect: ITAT Delhi
summary: ITAT Delhi upheld deletion of Section 56(2)(viib) addition, holding the provision applied on share allotment and the assessee could choose the Rule 11UA valuation method. The post AO Cannot Reject Assessee’s Chosen Rule 11UA Valuation Without Defect: ITAT Delhi appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT Delhi held that an Assessing Officer (AO) cannot reject an assessee's chosen valuation method under Rule 11UA for Section 56(2)(viib) additions without identifying a specific defect. The ruling affirmed the assessee's right to select the appropriate valuation methodology for share allotments.

title: Share Valuation Must Follow Rule 11UA Applicable for Relevant AY: Delhi HC
summary: Delhi HC upheld deletion of Section 56 addition after holding Rule 11UA applicable for AY 2014-15 could not be replaced with the amended formula effective from AY 2018-19. The post Share Valuation Must Follow Rule 11UA Applicable for Relevant AY: Delhi HC appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The Delhi High Court affirmed that share valuation under Rule 11UA must adhere to the formula applicable for the relevant Assessment Year (AY 2014-15), rejecting the application of a later amended formula from AY 2018-19. This decision ensures that valuation rules are applied prospectively according to their effective dates.

title: WhatsApp Chats Accepted as Evidence, On-Money Addition Limited to 25%: ITAT Mumbai
summary: ITAT Mumbai upheld taxation of only 25% profit on alleged on-money receipts, rejected Section 69C addition of entire amount and sustained reassessment. The post WhatsApp Chats Accepted as Evidence, On-Money Addition Limited to 25%: ITAT Mumbai appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT Mumbai accepted WhatsApp chats as evidence and limited the 'on-money' addition to 25% of the alleged receipts, rejecting the Section 69C addition of the entire amount. This ruling sustained the reassessment with a reduced addition based on the evidence presented.

title: Section 50C Amendment Retrospective for Agreement Date Valuation: ITAT Mumbai
summary: ITAT Mumbai upheld application of the retrospective Section 50C amendment, directing capital gains computation using stamp duty value on the agreement date. The post Section 50C Amendment Retrospective for Agreement Date Valuation: ITAT Mumbai appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT Mumbai upheld the retrospective application of the Section 50C amendment, directing capital gains computation using the stamp duty value on the agreement date. This decision allows for a revised calculation of capital gains based on the property's valuation at the time of agreement.

title: No DAPE Where Indian AE is at Arm’s Length: Bangalore ITAT
summary: ITAT Bangalore deleted the DAPE-based business income addition where the Indian AE was at arm's length and remanded the refund interest issue to the AO. The post No DAPE Where Indian AE is at Arm’s Length: Bangalore ITAT appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT Bangalore ruled that no Dependent Agent Permanent Establishment (DAPE) addition to business income is warranted if the Indian Associated Enterprise (AE) operates at arm's length. This decision underscores the importance of the arm's length principle in determining the existence and profit attribution of a DAPE.

title: Appeal Dismissed; HC Order on Section 153D Approval Upheld: SC
summary: SC dismissed the SLP and declined to interfere with the HC judgment holding Section 153D approval was granted mechanically without application of mind. The post Appeal Dismissed; HC Order on Section 153D Approval Upheld: SC appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: India's Supreme Court dismissed a Special Leave Petition, upholding a High Court judgment that found approval under Section 153D was granted mechanically. This ruling emphasizes the need for proper application of mind by authorities in tax assessment procedures.
